accepted.title: Cooptrack: Exploring End-to-End Learning for Efficient Cooperative Sequential Perception

accepted.title: QCTF: A Quantized Communication and Transferable Fusion Framework for Multi-Agent Collaborative Perception

accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception with quantized feature communication and transferable fusion, evaluated on sim and real datasets.

accepted.title: CoRange: Collaborative Range-Aware Adaptive Fusion for Multi-Agent Perception
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception with range-aware feature sharing, evaluated on simulated and real-world datasets.

accepted.title: DATA: Domain-And-Time Alignment for High-Quality Feature Fusion in Collaborative Perception
accepted.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
accepted.reason: Feature-level collaborative perception with domain and time alignment, evaluated on three CP datasets.

accepted.title: Adaptive Fusion of LiDAR Features for 3D Object Detection in Autonomous Driving

accepted.venue: IEEE transactions on circuits and systems for video technology (Print)
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative detection (SCOPE++) aggregating spatio-temporal cross-agent features, evaluated on multiple LiDAR CP datasets.

accepted.venue: 2025 IEEE 5th International Conference on Electronic Technology, Communication and Information (ICETCI)

accepted.venue: 2025 IEEE International Annual Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Science (CSIS-IAC)
accepted.reason: Vehicle-infrastructure collaborative perception with deformable-attention multimodal feature fusion, experimentally validated.

accepted.venue: Transportation Research Part C: Emerging Technologies
accepted.reason: Multi-vehicle cooperative perception sharing camera feeds/sensor readings with helper-vehicle selection, evaluated on CARLA pedestrian detection.

accepted.venue: IEEE/RJS International Conference on Intelligent RObots and Systems
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception sharing object queries, evaluated on OPV2V and V2V4Real.

accepted.title: A Lightweight Two-Stage Multivehicle Feature Fusion Method Guided by Global Feature
accepted.reason: Multi-vehicle collaborative perception with two-stage feature fusion, evaluated on simulated and real-world datasets.

accepted.title: STAMP: Scalable Task And Model-agnostic Collaborative Perception
accepted.reason: Task- and model-agnostic collaborative perception sharing BEV features among heterogeneous agents, evaluated on sim and real datasets.

accepted.title: Collaborative Multimodal Fusion Network for Multiagent Perception
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception fusing LiDAR and camera across agents, evaluated on OPV2V and V2XSet.

accepted.title: Perception for Connected Autonomous Vehicles under Adverse Weather Conditions

accepted.reason: V2V cooperative perception with spatial-wise feature fusion under foggy weather, evaluated on OPV2V.

accepted.title: Multi-Agent Collaborative Perception via Motion-Aware Robust Communication Network
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative 3D detection robust to pose/motion noise (MRCNet), evaluated on collaborative detection datasets.

accepted.title: Infrastructure-Assisted Collaborative Perception in Automated Valet Parking: A Safety Perspective
accepted.reason: Infrastructure-assisted BEV-feature collaborative perception fusing roadside and onboard sensors, evaluated on synthetic AVP dataset.

accepted.reason: Vehicle-infrastructure collaboration for intersection blind-spot perception with feature transmission, evaluated on DAIR-V2X.

accepted.reason: Vehicles share and fuse perception results with neighbors for collaborative perception, evaluated on OPV2V and others.

accepted.title: A Sparse BEV Feature Transmission Algorithm with Delay Compensation for Vehicle-Infrastructure Cooperative Perception
accepted.reason: Vehicle-infrastructure cooperative perception sharing sparse BEV features with delay compensation, evaluated by simulation.

accepted.reason: V2X heterogeneous multiscale cooperative perception fusing LiDAR/camera BEV features, evaluated on OPV2V and DAIR-V2X.

accepted.reason: Optimizes infrastructure sensor placement to enhance cooperative vehicle-infrastructure perception, evaluated via co-simulation with occupancy metrics.

accepted.reason: Multi-vehicle cooperative BEV perception sending camera data to edge server fusing local views, with motion prediction; experimentally evaluated.

accepted.title: Efficient Collaborative Multi-Agent Driving via Cross-Attention and Concise Communication
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative driving sharing concise state info via cross-attention communication, evaluated on a driving simulator.

accepted.title: CooPercept: Cooperative Perception for 3D Object Detection of Autonomous Vehicles
accepted.reason: Multi-vehicle cooperative 3D detection fusing LiDAR and camera features across vehicles, evaluated on KITTI and OPV2V.

accepted.title: BadMDA: Towards Backdoor Injection during Domain Adaptation to Collapse Multi-Agent Perception
accepted.reason: Studies backdoor attacks on multi-agent collaborative perception via domain adaptation in 3D detection, with extensive experiments.

accepted.reason: V2X cooperative 3D detection integrating multi-agent features via state-space models, evaluated on V2X/V2V datasets.

accepted.title: Bridging Infrastructures and Vehicles: A Cooperative Framework for Fusing Heterogeneous Future Trajectory Prediction
accepted.reason: Cooperative infrastructure-vehicle trajectory prediction fusing shared predicted trajectories, evaluated against offline/online baselines.

accepted.venue: IEEE transactions on circuits and systems for video technology (Print)
accepted.reason: Roadside cross-infrastructure cooperative perception with Mamba-based feature fusion, evaluated on DAIR-V2X and RCooper.

accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception exchanging hard-instance features via query-based intermediate fusion, evaluated on V2X-Real and DAIR-V2X.

accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception aligning heterogeneous features via domain separation, evaluated on CP detection datasets.

accepted.title: Residual Vector Quantization For Communication-Efficient Multi-Agent Perception
accepted.venue: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative perception compressing intermediate features via residual vector quantization, evaluated on DAIR-V2X.

accepted.title: CoST: Efficient Collaborative Perception from Unified Spatiotemporal Perspective
accepted.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
accepted.reason: Collaborative perception fusing multi-agent and multi-time observations in unified spatiotemporal space, compatible with existing CP methods.

accepted.reason: Robustness benchmark for collaborative detection under corruptions, introduces OPV2V-C, V2XSet-C, DAIR-V2X-C and evaluates 10 CP models.

accepted.title: LangCoop: Collaborative Driving with Language

accepted.reason: Multi-agent collaborative driving with vision-language inter-agent communication, evaluated in closed-loop CARLA.

accepted.reason: V2X feature-level collaborative 3D detection addressing domain gaps and pose errors, evaluated on DAIR-V2X.

accepted.title: One is Plenty: A Polymorphic Feature Interpreter for Immutable Heterogeneous Collaborative Perception
accepted.reason: Heterogeneous collaborative perception interpreting exchanged intermediate features across agents, evaluated on OPV2V.
